1: What What work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.
2: How How thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.
3: Because thou hadst a favour unto them.
4: Thou art my King, O God: Command deliverances for Jacob.
5: Through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.
6: Neither shall my sword save me.
7: And hast put them to shame that hated us.
8: And praise thy name for ever. Selah. Selah.
9: And goest not forth with our armies.
10: And they which hate us spoil for themselves.
11: And hast scattered us among the heathen.
12: And dost not increase thy wealth thy wealth by their price.
13: A scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
14: A shaking of the head among the people.
15: And the shame of my face hath covered me,
16: By reason of the enemy and avenger.
17: Neither have we dealt falsely in thy covenant.
18: Neither have our steps declined from thy way;
19: And covered us with the shadow of death.
20: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;
21: Shall not God search this out? For he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
22: We are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
23: Arise, cast us us not off for ever.
24: And And forgettest our affliction and our oppression?
25: Our belly cleaveth unto the earth.
26: And redeem us for thy mercies' sake.
